message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should reject unknown item type (e.g. 'broomstick') with non-zero exit and error to stderr"
Expected behavior: claimOffice should throw an error when given an unknown item type
Current error: "expected [Function] to throw an error" — function doesn't throw, silently returns NaN
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Add minimal validation: when looking up an item's base premium, if the type is not found in BASE_PREMIUMS, throw an Error. This is the simplest way to make the test pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text:
The test expects `claimOffice` to throw when given an unknown item type like `'broomstick'`, but currently it silently proceeds and produces `NaN` because `BASE_PREMIUMS['broomstick']` is `undefined`.

The minimal fix: add a check when looking up an item's base premium. If the type is not found in `BASE_PREMIUMS`, throw an `Error`.

content.text:
All 30 tests pass (22 remaining as todo).

**Green Phase Complete:**

**Implementation**: Added a guard at the top of the item processing loop in `claimOffice` that checks whether `item.type` exists as a key in `BASE_PREMIUMS`. If not, it throws an `Error` with a message identifying the unknown type.

**Result**: All tests now pass (30 passing, 22 todo).

**Approach**: This is the minimal change -- a single `if` statement with a `throw` added before any premium calculation occurs. It catches unknown types at the earliest point where `BASE_PREMIUMS` is consulted, preventing the silent `NaN` propagation. No other logic was changed or added.

The relevant file is `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-4/src/claim-office.ts` (line 35-37 contain the new validation).

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
